figures.caption: Figure 1: Three paradigms for KV cache sequence-length reduction. (a) Eviction selects a subset of original KV pairs. (b) Merge combines similar pairs, modifying values but remaining anchored to original positions. (c) KVSCULPT distills the compress zone into k unconstrained pairs (orange) freely optimized in Rd via L-BFGS (keys) and least squares (values). All three keep the retain zone (green) intact. The key distinction is the degree of freedom: from discrete subset to weighted combination to fully continuous optimization.
